subject: Computer Networking
title: Terabit Optical Networks
description: A fast breakdown of Terabit Optical Networks for builders.

hook.text: One fiber strand carries 100 terabits per second. We're nowhere near fiber's physical limits.

buildup.text: WDM sends many colors of light through one fiber. Each color carries its own data stream.

discovery.text: Coherent optics encode data in amplitude, phase, and polarization at once. More dimensions, more data.

twist.text: Hollow-core fiber replaces glass with air. Light moves faster, cutting latency 30%. Great for trading.

climax.text: Multi-core fiber packs seven cores into one cable. Seven times the capacity, no new routes.

punchline.text: Fiber has vast untapped capacity. The bottleneck is electronics, not glass.

quiz.question: How does wavelength-division multiplexing increase fiber capacity?
options: By sending multiple colors of light, each carrying separate data, through one fiber
By making the fiber thicker to carry more light
By increasing the speed of light inside the fiber
